title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) - Easy & Authentic Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) with our easy, step-by-step recipe. This hearty, healthy, and flavorful Middle Eastern soup is perfect for any meal!

Ingredients for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup
How to Make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup: Step-by-Step
Pro Tips for the Best Adas Soup
seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh cilantro and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a bowl of this incredibly flavorful and healthy Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, a staple in Middle Eastern cuisine.

description: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, is a comforting and nutritious staple in Middle Eastern cuisine. This vibrant soup is bursting with the earthy flavors of red lentils, fresh vegetables, and a bright kick of lemon. It is a hearty and wholesome dish, perfect for a warming meal any time of year.

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Vegetables
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Set aside to drain. Prepare all your vegetables by finely chopping the onion and carrot, dicing the potato, and mincing the garlic.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and carrot and sauté for 5-7 minutes until they soften and become translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Stir in the rinsed and drained red lentils, diced potato, cumin powder, and dried mint. Pour in the vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Allow the soup to simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and potatoes are very tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking to the bottom of the pot.

instructions.title: Blend (Optional)
instructions.text: For a smoother consistency, use an immersion blender to partially or fully blend the soup directly in the pot. If you prefer a chunky texture, you can skip this step or mash some of the lentils and potatoes with a spoon.

instructions.title: Season and Finish
instructions.text: Stir in the fresh lemon juice, salt, and black pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed. The lemon is crucial for the authentic bright flavor of Adas.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro and serve immediately with extra lemon wedges on the side. It pairs wonderfully with pita bread.

tips: For a richer flavor, toast the cumin and dried mint lightly in a dry pan for 30 seconds before adding them to the soup. This enhances their aromatic oils.
Always use fresh lemon juice; it is crucial for the authentic bright and tangy flavor of Adas. Bottled lemon juice will not yield the same vibrant result.
Adas thickens considerably as it cools. When reheating, you may need to add a splash of hot water or vegetable broth to achieve your desired consistency.
article: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is a beloved Middle Eastern Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
